XMVDecoder::EnableAudioStream

Tells the decoder to play one of the audio tracks in the Xbox Media Video (XMV) file.

HRESULT EnableAudioStream(
    DWORD AudioStream,
    DWORD Flags,
    DSMIXBINS *pMixBins,
    IDirectSoundStream **ppStream
);

Parameters

AudioStream
[in] Number of the audio stream to enable.
Flags
[in] Used when creating the stream. It must be zero, or one of the following flags ORed together:
Value Description
DSSTREAMCAPS_CTRL3D The stream will be a 3D HRTF stream. Can be set only for streams with a mono format.
DSSTREAMCAPS_LOCDEFER [Do not use. This flag does not function properly.] The stream will not allocate a hardware voice until it is played.
pMixBins
[in] Address of a DSMIXBINS structure that contains the output mixbin identifiers and volume pairs. Can be set to NULL, which specifies that the default mixbin volume pairs are used. (See Mixbin Volume Pair Combinations for more information about the default mixbin volume pairs.)
ppStream
[out] Address of a variable that will receive a pointer to the IDirectSoundStream interface. If the title doesn't intend to manually adjust the sound parameters, ppStream may be set to NULL.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

This function can be called multiple times for different streams; allowing streams to be played simultaneouly. At least one stream must be enabled before audio will play. The video output will be synchronized to the very first audio stream that is enabled.

A 5.1 stream compressed with ADPCM actually is encoded as three consecutive streams: the first for the front left-right channels, the second for both the center and low-frequency channels, and the third containing the rear left-right channels. These will be indicated by the presence of one of the XMVAUDIODESC_51_ADPCM flags in the XMVAUDIO_DESC structure returned from GetAudioDescriptor.

If this API is called on the first stream of an ADPCM 5.1 set (front left-right stream) with no flags, NULL passed in for pMixBins and NULL passed in for ppStream, then the decoder will automatically enable all three streams. Otherwise, the caller needs to make sure that each stream is individually enabled.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xmv.h.

Import Library: Use Xmv.lib.

See Also

DSMIXBINS, IDirectSoundStream
